heading: Reimbursement of States for costs of incarcerating illegal aliens and certain Cuban nationals
label: 1365 Reimbursement of States for costs of incarcerating illegal aliens and certain Cuban nationals
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Immigration Reform and Control Act of 1986, and not as part of the Immigration and Nationality Act which comprises this chapter.

Regulations, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 103–317, title VIII108 Stat. 1778Aug. 26, 1994“That the Attorney General shall promulgate regulations to (a) prescribe requirements for program participation eligibility for States, (b) require verification by States of the eligible incarcerated population data with the Immigration and Naturalization Service, (c) prescribe a formula for distributing assistance to eligible States, and (d) award assistance to eligible States”.[For abolition of Immigration and Naturalization Service, transfer of functions, and treatment of related references, see note set out under .]section 1551 of this title
amendments: Regulations, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 103–317, title VIII108 Stat. 1778Aug. 26, 1994“That the Attorney General shall promulgate regulations to (a) prescribe requirements for program participation eligibility for States, (b) require verification by States of the eligible incarcerated population data with the Immigration and Naturalization Service, (c) prescribe a formula for distributing assistance to eligible States, and (d) award assistance to eligible States”.[For abolition of Immigration and Naturalization Service, transfer of functions, and treatment of related references, see note set out under .]section 1551 of this title

children.heading: Reimbursement of States
children.label: a Reimbursement of States
children.text: Subject to the amounts provided in advance in appropriation Acts, the Attorney General shall reimburse a State for the costs incurred by the State for the imprisonment of any illegal alien or Cuban national who is convicted of a felony by such State.

children.heading: Illegal aliens convicted of a felony
children.label: b Illegal aliens convicted of a felony

children.text: whose most recent entry into the United States was without inspection, or

children.label: A
children.text: whose period of authorized stay as a nonimmigrant expired, or

children.label: B
children.text: whose unlawful status was known to the Government,

children.heading: Marielito Cubans convicted of a felony
children.label: c Marielito Cubans convicted of a felony

children.text: was allowed by the Attorney General to come to the United States in 1980,
after such arrival committed any violation of State or local law for which a term of imprisonment was imposed, and

children.label: A
children.text: for permanent or temporary residence, or

children.label: B
children.text: under the terms of an immigrant visa or a nonimmigrant visa issued,

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: “State” defined
children.label: e “State” defined
children.text: The term “State” has the meaning given such term in .section 1101(a)(36) of this title
